title: Shinji in the eyes of Anno
display_title: Shinji in the eyes of Anno
wikitext:
[]: Shinji in the eyes of Anno.             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:14 pm I don't know if only I perceive it that way, but ever since i first watched this series, I've only felt very confused about the way Shinji is portrayed. Sometimes the series really shows that the world is cruel towards Shinji, but other times the series is really saying "look at how pathetic this guy is". I don't know if Anno was kind of confused himself, maybe he just felt different ways, maybe it's also becasuse he´s kind of pedantic. But it's like, sometimes you feel like this series supports Shinji, and the viewer is supposed to sympathyze with him, other times it seems like Anno is trying to portray him as the worst thing ever. Have you noticed this?

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:30 pm Shinji is a part of Anno The part that he hates. And it shows.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:38 pm Stillborn wrote: Shinji is a part of Anno The part that he hates. And it shows.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:40 pm When he is shown to be pathetic, it isn't so that we can despise him, but so that we can feel the sadness of observing that someone can be in that state. Sympathy again, but from a different angle.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:43 pm pwhodges wrote: When he is shown to be pathetic, it isn't so that we can despise him, but so that we can feel the sadness of observing that someone can be in that state. Sympathy again, but from a different angle.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:49 pm Not to hate him, no. But then I'm the sort of person who if he comes across someone unlikable feels sadness at how they had come to be like that more strongly than the dislike of how they are. Especially if it is only a part of their character - I find it much easier to dislike Gendou, because I see no redeeming features in his behaviour. And given that he represents aspects of Anno, then Anno as a recovering depressive might have a similar view; in my experience people who truly hate part of themselves are less likely to improve that part than those who take a more rounded view.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:51 pm kuribo-04 wrote: So you didn't feel as if the series portrayed Shinji in a bad way? Never?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Feb 11, 2014 6:55 pm Or to summarise: "There but for the grace of God* go I" (and if you think you don't you're probably wrong). * or $DeityEquivalent

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 7:19 pm I like Shinji, but what I'm not sure about is what Anno was trying to do. He once said he realized how stupid he is after making Eva, maybe he was showing how stupid Shinji is. I'm not sure if I'm explaining myself. Sometimes I'm not sure if the point he is making is "don't hate yourself, you aren't alone, etc." or "stop being such a horrible otaku, you are pathetic if you behave like this". For me it seems like he does both. The second one is an angry approach.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Feb 11, 2014 7:24 pm pwhodges wrote: When he is shown to be pathetic, it isn't so that we can despise him, but so that we can feel the sadness of observing that someone can be in that state. Sympathy again, but from a different angle.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Tue Feb 11, 2014 7:45 pm It's... Look at Anno. He can make an anime series about how he's overly self-depreciating, and then depreciate it. You watch the scenes with Shinji's cello, and then you watch that documentary with his home town. "Do you like the anime you make?" "Hm. It depends. Thee's parts I like, and parts I don't" "Which ones do you nt like?" "The one's I participated in." It's exactly the same as the Cello thing... except, to write episode 15, he needed to be aware of that. He's aware of that but it still happens to him and he's stuck with the sight of himself.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Feb 11, 2014 7:49 pm Kendrix wrote: and not to reject new things beyond your favored corner of the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Tue Feb 11, 2014 10:30 pm IIRC, Anno stated somewhere that Shinji was partially made up of little bits of his (Anno's) own personality.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Feb 12, 2014 12:28 am kuribo-04 wrote: I've always loved that.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Wed Feb 12, 2014 12:53 am No no I get the reasoning, I was just disheartened that I don't feel it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Feb 12, 2014 9:18 am CJD wrote: No no I get the reasoning, I was just disheartened that I don't feel it.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Wed Feb 12, 2014 9:59 am Kendrix wrote: Yeah, from the common impression/perception of him you'd think he's this pretentious intense artist person, but when you actually read interviews, he's oddly cute. Or well, as cute as grown men with neckbeards in shorts can get.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Thu Feb 13, 2014 12:17 am kuribo-04 wrote: BTW, when was that coming out?

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Thu Feb 13, 2014 1:50 am Kendrix wrote: Yeah, from the common impression/perception of him you'd think he's this pretentious intense artist person, but when you actually read interviews, he's oddly cute. Or well, as cute as grown men with neckbeards in shorts can get.

[]:             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Thu Feb 13, 2014 11:24 am cyharding wrote: The release date for North America is the 25th of this month. I'll probably make my order before the end of next week.
